validators.name: HTTP boundary hardening pull request workflow
isolated Codex CLI post-merge implementation review
validators.summary: No actionable regression was found after independent review of authentication, authorization, scope, audit identity, bearer transport, and the complete validation suite.

validators.name: CodeRabbit pull request status context
validators.summary: The required status completed successfully; no review findings were posted because the service reported a rate limit. Independent Codex reviews supplied the substantive review lane.

themes: unauthenticated legacy HTTP mutations and caller-controlled audit identity
remote plaintext bearer transport
authentication before routing across the complete /v1 namespace
daemon-only loopback validation without breaking stdio MCP configuration
closure review provenance and premature next-Sprint authorization
non-circular separation of closure evidence from external PR publication controls
immutable binding between independent review and the exact closure candidate
preservation of capability gates established by authoritative prior receipts
chronologically credible amendments to audit evidence
protocol downgrade prevention and minimal attestation diffs
squash-stable two-phase publication and Sprint-specific roadmap ownership
published-main ancestry enforcement and decision-record governance
immutable CI base injection and stale-main rejection
unconditional protocol-v2 candidates with legacy receipt compatibility
content-addressed grandfathering of immutable historical receipts
content-preserving receipt promotion and canonical successor authorization
irreversible closure state and mandatory Git-history verification
type-strict canonical JSON preservation during receipt promotion
unique receipt introduction and canonical ASCII Sprint identity
non-shallow Git history and lossless numeric-token preservation
full merge-history inspection for irreversible receipt introduction

acceptance.criterion: a client can propose, inspect, approve, and cancel a synthetic Sprint
free-form chat text cannot bypass a pending approval contract
every tool action emits correlated audit events

signals: structured success and failure audit events include tool, actor, correlation, causation, and replay identity
HTTP faults distinguish unauthenticated, forbidden, conflict, invalid input, unavailable dependency, and internal failure
readiness fails when the durable dependency or canonical schema is unavailable
command receipts can be verified mechanically against domain and audit event evidence
